structure.title: Preface
structure.summary: The preface of the document "Requirements on Operating System Interface.pdf" provides an overview of its status and history within the AUTOSAR framework. It introduces key concepts such as the document's identification number, part of the Adaptive Platform in the AUTOSAR Standard, and its release version R25-11. The section outlines important changes over time, including updates to C++17, additions of new requirements like tracing support, and deletions of obsolete ones. It also includes a disclaimer regarding the use and commercial exploitation of the document's content, emphasizing that it is intended for automotive applications only.

structure.title: Scope of Document
structure.summary: This section of the document outlines the scope and purpose of the requirements specified for the Operating System within the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. It defines that these requirements pertain specifically to the Foundation part of the platform, which is a crucial component in ensuring the proper functioning of the adaptive software architecture. The document establishes key relationships between the operating system and other elements of the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform, particularly focusing on how the OS must interface with the platform's foundation layer. While no specific tables are mentioned, it sets the stage for detailed technical specifications that will be elaborated upon in subsequent sections.

structure.title: Conventions to be used
structure.summary: This section of "Requirements on Operating System Interface.pdf" outlines the conventions to be used when interfacing with an operating system. It introduces key terms such as "interface layer," which refers to the software component responsible for communication between applications and the OS, and "API (Application Programming Interface)," which defines the methods available for interaction. The section emphasizes that these conventions ensure consistency and predictability in how different software components interact with the OS. While no specific tables are mentioned, it is implied that detailed specifications of these interfaces will be provided elsewhere in the document to guide developers in implementing compliant code.

nodes.title: Document Conventions
nodes.summary: This section of the "Requirements on Operating System Interface.pdf" document outlines the conventions for representing and expressing requirements in AUTOSAR documents. It specifies that these representations should adhere to a table format detailed in [TPS_STDT_00078] and use verbal forms defined in [TPS_STDT_00053] to indicate obligations, as per the Standardization Template provided. These conventions ensure consistency and clarity in requirement documentation, facilitating effective communication among stakeholders involved in AUTOSAR projects.

nodes.title: Requirements Guidelines
nodes.summary: The "Requirements Guidelines" section in the document "Requirements on Operating System Interface.pdf" outlines the fundamental principles and standards for defining requirements related to operating system interfaces. It introduces key concepts such as interface specifications, compatibility levels, and performance benchmarks. The guidelines establish important relationships between different components of the OS interface, emphasizing the need for clear definitions and constraints to ensure seamless integration and interoperability. While no specific tables are mentioned in this excerpt, the section likely provides detailed criteria for measuring compliance and performance, which are crucial for developers and system integrators working with operating systems.

nodes.title: Requirements status
nodes.summary: This section outlines the status of certain requirements related to the operating system interface as detailed in "Requirements on Operating System Interface.pdf." It identifies specific requirements—[RS_OSI_00204], [RS_OSI_00208], and [RS_OSI_00209]—that are currently not included in the current release but may be considered for future updates. These requirements pertain to functionalities that could potentially be modified or enhanced, indicating an ongoing development process where these features might evolve before being integrated into a subsequent version of the document.

structure.title: Acronyms and abbreviations
structure.summary: This section of the document "Requirements on Operating System Interface.pdf" outlines a glossary of acronyms and abbreviations pertinent to operating systems and their interfaces within the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. Key concepts include definitions for terms such as Operating System, which manages processes and provides hardware access, and Operating System Interface (OSI), which specifies how adaptive applications interact with the platform. The section also references other technical terms like Execution Management, Adaptive Application, and Task, linking them to their detailed descriptions in separate documents. These relationships highlight the interconnected nature of these components within the AUTOSAR framework, emphasizing the importance of precise terminology for understanding system interactions and requirements.

structure.title: Requirements Specification
structure.summary: This section of the "Requirements on Operating System Interface.pdf" document outlines all necessary requirements for selecting an operating system suitable for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. It introduces key concepts related to the operating system's compatibility and functionality within this platform, emphasizing the need for specific features and performance characteristics. The chapter also describes important relationships between these requirements and the overall architecture of the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform, highlighting any constraints or processes that must be considered during the selection process. While no tables are explicitly mentioned in the provided content, it is implied that detailed technical specifications and criteria may be presented elsewhere in the document to support the outlined requirements.

nodes.title: Functional Overview
nodes.summary: This section provides an overview of the Operating System's role in managing run-time resources for applications on the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. It highlights that both Adaptive Applications and Functional Clusters (which are also applications) rely on this OS, with Execution Management working alongside it to initialize the platform and manage application start-up/shutdown. The document categorizes requirements into two groups: those directly supporting Adaptive Applications and those essential for implementing Functional Clusters, particularly noting that certain mechanisms must be handled by the Operating System due to their complexity or inefficiency elsewhere.

nodes.title: Functional Requirements
nodes.summary: The "Functional Requirements" section in the document "Requirements on Operating System Interface.pdf" outlines the specific functionalities that an operating system interface must support to meet the project's needs. It introduces key concepts such as user interaction protocols, process management, and resource allocation mechanisms. The section describes important relationships between these components, emphasizing how they interact to ensure seamless operation. Additionally, it includes relevant technical details from accompanying tables, detailing constraints on response times for various operations and specifying the minimum required performance metrics for different interface functionalities.

nodes.title: Assumption of Use
nodes.summary: This section of "Assumption of Use" in the document "Requirements on Operating System Interface.pdf" outlines the execution environment for Adaptive Applications within the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. Key concepts include assumptions about properties of these applications, which are used to derive further requirements and provide guidance for developers. The section emphasizes that while not strict requirements, these assumptions help define the scope of the specification document and guide the development process. Although no specific tables are mentioned in this excerpt, it implies that detailed technical requirements on the Operating System for Processes are defined elsewhere in the document.

nodes.title: General Requirements
nodes.summary: This section of the document outlines general requirements for the Operating System Interface (OSI) on the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. It emphasizes POSIX PSE51 compliance as the foundation for adaptive application processes, supporting C++ development, and enabling processes to react to external stimuli from devices. The OSI must also facilitate the startup of Execution Management as the first process, which is crucial for managing all Processes in the platform. Key technical details include adherence to specific POSIX profiles and support for particular C++ versions, ensuring compatibility and functionality across different application scenarios.

nodes.title: Operating System Requirements
nodes.summary: This section of the document outlines critical requirements for an operating system (OS) in the context of a multi-process environment, focusing on resource management and security. Key concepts include memory budgeting, CPU time allocation, process binding to cores, secure access control, application isolation, shared code/data mechanisms, restricted functionality access, error handling, and observability of scheduling information. The OS must ensure that processes can run independently while providing tools for system integrators to configure resource budgets and manage security risks. Additionally, the OS should support efficient memory usage through shared objects and provide mechanisms for tracing and exporting low-level scheduling details to applications. These requirements are interrelated, with constraints such as ensuring schedulability and preventing unauthorized access, and they collectively aim to enhance the robustness, safety, and security of the system.

nodes.title: Non-Functional Requirements
nodes.summary: The section "Non-Functional Requirements" in the document "Requirements on Operating System Interface.pdf" appears to be currently empty, indicating that there are no specific non-functional requirements outlined for the operating system interface at this stage. Non-functional requirements typically encompass aspects such as performance, security, usability, and reliability criteria that must be met by the system. In a typical section of this nature, one might expect to find definitions or descriptions of these key concepts, along with any constraints or processes related to their implementation. However, since no content is present in this particular section, it suggests that either the requirements have not yet been defined or they are being deferred for later consideration.

structure.title: References
structure.summary: This section of the document "Requirements on Operating System Interface.pdf" outlines the references and change history for requirements related to operating system interfaces in AUTOSAR (Automotive Open System Architecture) releases. Key concepts include POSIX PSE51 compliance, support for C++, and mechanisms for memory and CPU time budgeting. The document details changes across multiple AUTOSAR releases, from R23-11 to R25-11, highlighting additions, deletions, and modifications in requirements such as process external stimulus handling, multi-process support, and error handling. Tables A.1 through A.5 provide a structured overview of these changes, illustrating the evolution of operating system interface standards over time.
